title: Understanding Angular Momentum Conservation
text: The angular momentum of the system is conserved because there are no external torques acting on it. This means that the initial angular momentum of the system is equal to the final angular momentum.

title: Initial Angular Momentum
text: Calculate the initial angular momentum of the system. Use the formula for angular momentum: $$L=I \omega$$, where I is the moment of inertia and ω is the angular velocity.

title: Calculate Initial Moment of Inertia
text: The initial moment of inertia of the system is the sum of the moment of inertia of the disk and the bug. Use the formula $$I_{disk}=\frac{1}{2} M R^2$$ and $$I_{bug}=m R^2$$.

title: Calculate Initial Angular Momentum
text: Using the initial moment of inertia and the initial angular velocity, calculate the initial angular momentum: $$L_{initial}=I_{initial} \omega_{initial}$$.

title: Final Moment of Inertia
text: When the bug reaches the center, its contribution to the moment of inertia becomes zero. The final moment of inertia is just that of the disk.

title: Calculate Final Angular Velocity
text: Using the conservation of angular momentum, $$L_{initial}=L_{final}$$, calculate the final angular velocity: $$\omega_{final}=\frac{L_{initial}}{I_{final}}$$.
